original_query: select  i_item_id,         s_state, grouping(s_state) g_state,         avg(ss_quantity) agg1,         avg(ss_list_price) agg2,         avg(ss_coupon_amt) agg3,         avg(ss_sales_price) agg4 from store_sales, customer_demographics, date_dim, store, item where ss_sold_date_sk = d_date_sk and       ss_item_sk = i_item_sk and       ss_store_sk = s_store_sk and       ss_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk and       cd_gender = 'F' and       cd_marital_status = 'W' and       cd_education_status = 'College' and       d_year = 1999 and       s_state = 'WI' and      i_category  = 'Music'  group by rollup (i_item_id, s_state)  order by i_item_id          ,s_state  limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_demographics AS (
    SELECT cd_demo_sk
    FROM customer_demographics
    WHERE cd_gender = 'F'
      AND cd_marital_status = 'W'
      AND cd_education_status = 'College'
),
filtered_dates AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_year = 1999
),
filtered_stores AS (
    SELECT s_store_sk, s_state
    FROM store
    WHERE s_state = 'WI'
),
filtered_items AS (
    SELECT i_item_sk, i_item_id
    FROM item
    WHERE i_category = 'Music'
)
SELECT 
    i.i_item_id, 
    s.s_state, 
    grouping(s.s_state) as g_state,
    avg(ss.ss_quantity) as agg1,
    avg(ss.ss_list_price) as agg2,
    avg(ss.ss_coupon_amt) as agg3,
    avg(ss.ss_sales_price) as agg4
FROM store_sales ss
JOIN filtered_demographics cd ON ss.ss_cdemo_sk = cd.cd_demo_sk
JOIN filtered_dates d ON ss.ss_sold_date_sk = d.d_date_sk
JOIN filtered_stores s ON ss.ss_store_sk = s.s_store_sk
JOIN filtered_items i ON ss.ss_item_sk = i.i_item_sk
GROUP BY ROLLUP (i.i_item_id, s.s_state)
ORDER BY i.i_item_id, s.s_state
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Use explicit JOIN syntax instead of comma-separated tables in the FROM clause for better readability and to help the query optimizer understand the join relationships.
Move filter conditions into the JOIN ON clauses to potentially help the optimizer apply filters earlier in the join process.
Use table aliases consistently throughout the query for better readability and maintenance.
